response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the benefits of the promotional week. The customer will pay less for each item, which is a great way to support local artisans. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will now cost $170 after the 15% discount. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will now cost $127.50 after the same discount. The total amount the customer pays for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. The customer receives a significant discount on both products, which is a win for both the shop and the factory workers. ***

The promotional week is a great opportunity for customers to support local businesses and enjoy a better price on their favorite items. The 15% discount on both the handcrafted table and the factory-made chairs is a great way to encourage more people to shop locally. The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, which totals $297.50. This is a great way to support the local factory workers and enjoy a more affordable price on handmade products. ***
